name: Karnabeet Makly (Deep-fried Cauliflower)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut away the thick stem at the base of the cauliflower and remove the green leaves. Break the florets off the center core and cut the core into 2-inch cubes.
2. Wash the florets and cubes under cold running water.
3. In a saucepan, bring 4 quarts of water and the salt to a boil over high heat.
4. Drop in the cauliflower and cook briskly, uncovered, for 10 minutes, or until the pieces are tender but still somewhat resistant to a small, sharp knife.
5. Drain the cauliflower in a sieve or colander.
6. Heat oil to 375°F in a heavy skillet that measures at least 10 inches deep, or heat oil to 375°F in an electric skillet.
7. Pat the cauliflower completely dry with paper towels.
8. Fry the cauliflower pieces in batches for about 15 minutes, or until golden brown on all sides.
9. Remove the fried cauliflower with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
10. Serve hot or at room temperature covered with taratoor.
